pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 3 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Travelling the equivalent of a round-the-world trip, explorer Steve Boyes and his team are undertaking a series of pioneering expeditions to find new species and establish ecological baselines of Africa’s greatest rivers. Their first journey took them 900 kilometres from the remote Angolan highlands to the border with Zambia following a tributary to the mighty Zambezi River. What they discover will help build ecological resilience and protect waters that millions of people and countless unique ecosystems depend on.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 3

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes is encouraging local scientific teams to help in his effort to survey Africa’s major rivers. Watch the video on rolex.org.

title.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 3

text.value: Travelling the equivalent of a round-the-world trip, explorer Steve Boyes and his team are undertaking a series of pioneering expeditions to find new species and establish ecological baselines of Africa’s greatest rivers. Their first journey took them 900 kilometres from the remote Angolan highlands to the border with Zambia following a tributary to the mighty Zambezi River. What they discover will help build ecological resilience and protect waters that millions of people and countless unique ecosystems depend on.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are working closely with communities in Bali to protect some of the world’s most biodiverse coral reefs.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Founder of the Coral Triangle Center, Rili Djohani has dedicated her life to protecting Nusa Penida's coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Mount Logan Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Deep within the ice on the summit of Mount Logan, Canada’s highest peak, are clues about the Earth’s climate and atmosphere spanning thousands of years. Scientist and high-altitude mountaineer Alison Criscitiello led the Rolex and National Geographic Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition to retrieve these layers of past climates. What they find will help us understand past changing climates, and what they mean for our future.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Mountaineer Alison Criscitiello is expanding ice core research to new latitudes through her expedition to Mount Logan. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

pageCaption.value: With support from Rolex and the National Geographic Society, climate scientists and Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews endured snowstorms and sub-zero temperatures to install a state-of-the-art weather station on the summit of one of Peru’s highest mountains.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

metadescription.value: Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews have successfully installed a weather station in the high mountains of the Andes. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Venturing into the remote Angolan wilderness, conservationist and National Geographic Fellow Steve Boyes led an expedition to increase scientific knowledge of the mighty Zambezi River, on which millions of people depend. The discoveries they have made will help find ways to protect these vital waterways as the climate changes.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes and his team have set out to scientifically document the unexplored Lungwevungu River. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Lungwevungu River Expedition Episode 2 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: The Zambezi River, home to one of the natural wonders of the world, Victoria Falls, is a life source for millions of people, across six countries. National Geographic Explorer Steve Boyes and his team have travelled over 900 kilometres from the remote Angolan highlands to the Zambian border to better understand the health of this incredible waterway. This is the first of the Great Spine of Africa expeditions that will establish ecological baselines to reveal how Africa’s great rivers are changing.

WaPageTitle.value: The Great Spine of Africa Expeditions: Lungwevungu River - Episode 2

metadescription.value: Conservationist Steve Boyes is collecting extensive data of the Lungwevungu River to create baselines for future study. Watch the video on rolex.org.
